title: Select Button
subtitle: Group of buttons for single (Radio) or multiple (Checkbox) selection

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: The simplest case accepts an array of strings. By default, it acts as a single-option selector (Radio).

modes.title: Selection modes
modes.description: Allows changing the button selection type between radiobutton and checkbox
modes.note: Switch to mode='checkbox' to allow selecting multiple options at once. The returned value will be an Array.

complex.title: Complex objects
complex.description: Supports arrays of objects with custom properties for label, value and disabled state.
complex.note: Map arbitrary object properties using labelBy, valueBy, iconBy and disabledBy.

icons.title: Icons
icons.description: Supports icon-only buttons for a compact toolbar-style button group.
icons.note: Remix Icon support. Use iconOnly='true' for a compact toolbar-style button group. If it is an object, you can map the icon property using iconBy.

colors.title: Colors and variants
colors.description: Compatible with the full variant system (Solid, Outline, Ghost) and NUI colors.

segmented.title: Segmented variant (iOS style)
segmented.description: Visual style similar to iOS Segmented Control, special and in addition to the basic NUI variant types. Ideal for view switchers (e.g.: Month / Year, List / Map).
segmented.note: Use variant='segmented' to apply this unique variant.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five predefined sizes that harmoniously scale the container, typography and icons.

width.title: Full width
width.description: Expands buttons to occupy all available space equally.
width.note: Use width='full' so each button expands and occupies equal width within the container.

api.title: API and reference
api.description: Complete documentation of inputs, outputs and advanced usage of the Select Button component.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Component properties that control its behavior and appearance.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the component during its lifecycle.

theming.title: Styles and customization
theming.description: Customize the appearance of the Select Button using CSS variables. Control spacing, colors, shadows and more.

general.title: General variables
general.description: CSS variables to customize the general style of the Select Button (default layout).

gap.description: Space between buttons in the default layout.

transition.description: CSS transition applied to all buttons.

segmented.title: Segmented layout variables
segmented.description: CSS variables specific to the segmented style (iOS-like). Controls the container, selected/unselected states and hover effects.

gap.description: Space between buttons in the segmented layout.

bg.description: Background color of the segmented container.

padding.description: Inner padding of the segmented container.

borderRadius.description: Corner radius of the segmented container.

hoverBg.description: Background of the button on hover state.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The Select Button exposes semantic roles depending on its selection mode (radio or checkbox).

roleRadiogroup.description: The container uses role="radiogroup" in single-selection mode to group mutually exclusive options.

roleGroup.description: The container uses role="group" in multiple-selection mode to group related checkboxes.

ariaDisabled.description: Reflects the disabled state of the entire group to assistive technologies.

roleRadio.description: Each button uses role="radio" in single-selection mode.

roleCheckbox.description: Each button uses role="checkbox" in multiple-selection mode.

ariaChecked.description: Indicates whether the option is currently selected.

ariaHiddenIcon.description: Decorative icons are hidden from assistive technologies with aria-hidden="true".

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: Each option button gets its accessible name from the option label. Optionally, the group container can receive a contextual label.
naming.note: Always provide meaningful labels for options. For icon-only buttons, ensure each option has a descriptive label assigned via labelBy.

button.description: The accessible name of each option button is derived from the visible option label text.

container.description: The group container does not receive an accessible name by default. Wrap it with a labelled parent element if context is required.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: The Select Button uses roving tabindex in radio mode and standard Tab navigation in checkbox mode.

tab.description: In radio mode, Tab moves focus to the selected option (or to the first if none is selected). In checkbox mode, Tab cycles through all options.

arrowNext.description: In radio mode, moves focus to the next option and selects it (wraps around).

arrowPrev.description: In radio mode, moves focus to the previous option and selects it (wraps around).

spaceEnter.description: In checkbox mode, toggles the selection of the focused option.

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: Set default values for all SelectButton instances in your application using the provideNUI() function. Any property defined here is automatically applied to every select button that does not override it via a direct input binding.
setup.note: Only the properties you want to override need to be defined. Properties not specified fall back to the library defaults.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: Full list of configurable properties through SelectButtonGlobalConfig, their types and the library defaults.

rows.color: Color theme applied to all select buttons by default
rows.size: Size applied to all select buttons by default
rows.variant: Visual variant applied to all select buttons by default
rows.width: Width behaviour applied to all select buttons by default

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: Values are resolved following a strict three-level priority chain. The first matching level wins: direct input > global config > library default.
priority.note: The priority chain guarantees that components always have a valid resolved value. A direct input always wins; if not provided, the global config value is used; if not configured either, the library default is applied.
